verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is a secreted ligand that circulates as a latent complex (mature peptide homodimer + LAP homodimer + LTBP). The LTBP-containing large latent complex is covalently cross-linked (via transglutaminase) to extracellular matrix or tethered to cell-surface receptors (GARP/LRRC32, LRRC33) on regulatory T cells, platelets, and macrophages, forming a wash-resistant cell-surface pool. This stable surface anchoring via TM scaffold partners (GARP/LRRC33) qualifies as `stable_surface_attachment`. Surface display is thus documented but restricted to specific cell types and activation states, making this contextual rather than constitutive.
